pageTitle: All cities with fence installation cost data | Woogoro
metaDescription: Fence installation cost data by city and state. Material, terrain slope, and HOA setback rules drive the per-linear-foot number more than length does.
ogTitle: Fence installation cost by city | Woogoro
ogDescription: Fence installation pricing by city. Wood vs vinyl vs aluminum, slope multiplier, and permit thresholds across metros.
schemaDescription: Directory of U.S. cities with 2026 fence installation cost data, organized by state, including dominant material and HOA-setback context per metro.
intro: Fence pricing is per-linear-foot times material times terrain. Wood (cedar, pressure-treated pine) dominates at $25–$45/foot installed, vinyl runs $30–$60, ornamental aluminum sits $40–$75, and hybrid composite is climbing fast in HOA-driven Sun Belt subdivisions. Slope is the silent multiplier — a 6-foot privacy fence on a 20° grade racks racked-panel installation premiums of 25–40% over flat pour-in-place posts. Permit thresholds vary too: most municipalities require a permit at heights above 6 feet, but a few coastal jurisdictions (parts of FL and CA) require it at 4 feet plus engineered wind-load drawings. Click into any metro for dominant fence material, permit threshold, HOA-driven setback rule, and the slope-multiplier you should expect on bids in that area.
